## [00:00 - 03:00]

Manual bidding is a great way of overriding the auction so that you can bypass whatever Facebook feel that your bid should be. So we've talked previously about the auction, so bid times estimated action rate plus user value. What manual bidding does is to override Facebook's automatic bidding. So there are many scenarios where you would want to use this and also somewhere it can actually cost you more as well. And in this training video I'm going to explain how manual bidding actually works in the auction to help you decide which strategy of the ones that we use in the BPM method is going to be right for you. And that's not just you know which manual bidding strategy to use. The bigger question is do you need manual bidding at all? If you've followed the BPM method all the way through and you've gone through your testing, your core for your avatar work, you've optimized your conversion rates, your auction costs and your average order value and your funnel is working as well as it can, generally you don't really need manual bidding. Now there are scenarios where you know you might want to bypass some of those steps because you've got big promotion coming up and manual bidding is great for that. But in many cases manual bidding introduces a high level of stress and a bigger need to manage your ad account. So the big part of this is just weighing up. Do you want to go into manual bidding mode and keep a closer eye on everything that's going on? Because now you're playing with the auction and overriding what Facebook are working on or are you happy to work on the other inputs into that auction algorithm and get it working more effectively? So this is how the auction actually works. On the left hand side of this little graph is your bid amount. So if you're auto bidding then Facebook control that. And on the right hand side is your user value. So the closer you are to the left of the user value, there's a lower chance of that person converting in your funnel or they have a lower value and potentially both as well. As you go up the user value, these people have a higher chance of converting so they're more like your ideal prospects and or have higher value so you know they'll buy more and things like that. This line here which I've drawn just illustrates the auction success line. So if your auction bid falls above this then you'll win the auction. If the auction bid falls below this then you'll lose the auction and this circle here represents your target audience. So bear in mind that very rarely will you try and target every single person on Facebook. So your target audience is then therefore defined by your ad set. So you say I want to look alike or I want to interest or even if you're abroad and you're put in a country you're

## [03:00 - 06:05]

still limiting who you're targeting. So your target audience will you know this is just as an illustration but it will sit somewhere here. So you can only ever target people here then what it costs them is dependent on this curve. That curve is based on many many factors you know your niche, how well your ad account and your structure has been set, who you're targeting, what your performance is on the ad account, other people in your market, are they manual bidding etc. There's lots of things that go into this but essentially what Facebook is doing is trying to win you as many bids as possible for the lowest cost because if you get great results you spend more with Facebook. Okay so that kind of makes sense from that perspective. Now when we look at the different kinds of audiences and the CPM I want to illustrate how CPM has or gives you a view of the type of people you're bidding for in the auction. When you're manual bidding especially if you go in and manual bid a lot higher than potentially what Facebook is bidding for you, your CPMs can increase so it can cost you more to reach these higher premium users and anyone on the kind of top right here is a premium user. So that means that potentially there's more competition for these people because they spend a lot, they buy a lot and you know if you can get into their newsfeed then you're going to get a high conversion rate and higher potential value off the end of it. However it costs more as well because more people are bidding for those type of users and also Facebook wants to protect those people from seeing bad ads. So here's an example let's say you've just launched your funnel whatever it is you're selling and over the last three four weeks you've been really struggling to get good conversion rates you're getting kind of negative scores on your ads for example and you're getting a bit frustrated because you're really sure that you have a good product or service and you want to just get it in front of the right people. In this scenario if you went in and ran manual bidding chances are not only would you pay more to reach these people you'd be penalized for reaching them as well which means you'll get a CPM penalty because your ad account scores are low. So your engagement scores on your ad are low maybe your page score is low maybe your conversion rates are low maybe the signals coming back from your website are really quite poor they all go into determining your CPM. So it doesn't mean you can't get your ad in front of that person but it does mean that you're potentially paying more than the next advertiser and this is a big part about manual bidding to understand is it's not literally just you know you bid higher and you can reach the person and get them into a funnel it's balancing out how how much Facebook wants you to see that user as well. So the analogy

## [06:05 - 09:05]

the example I often share about manual bidding is it's kind of like eBay. So if you've seen the eBay auction model you see your products there might be a start price and then you bid into that auction. So let's take an example of a mobile phone the seller has listed it as a simple auction so there's no buy it now there's no lowest value etc so you can just bid whatever you want. Now it's a phone that you personally value at let's say $250 retail price 400 let's say so you're going into this auction even though nobody has placed a bid if you start off with a bid of $250 it doesn't automatically mean that you're paying $250 the way eBay works is you'll bid up to a certain level and then if other people bid they may or may not win that auction or at least become the kind of highest bidder unless they go above 250. So for example if you bid $250 and someone else had bid $50 eBay would automatically come in and bid a higher value so it might be 52 or 55 then someone else might look at that and say all right so this guy is increasing their bid I'm going to try 60 eBay automatically puts in 65 because eBay you've told eBay already that I'm prepared to pay up to $250 for this auction so that's an example of a really high manual bid in Facebook where you say I really want to pay this top top amount I'm prepared to go all in and pay this this higher fee for this user so taking it back into the Facebook world if for example you go into manual bid and there's different types of manual bid strategies which I'll cover off as well if you go in at a really really insanely high bid level chances are you'll win the auction however even though you might reach the user you could be overpaying for that user which makes that user unprofitable so you know one of the questions may be well I just want to win all the options and so I want to bid crazy amounts and win all the options well good luck to you because it doesn't mean that A you're going to automatically reach the right person at the right time and B it doesn't mean you're going to get a good value off the back of it as well because although you're now reaching these premium users if you're paying too much for them then it doesn't matter because you're you're going to create a negative return on Aspend or negative CPA anyway so one of the key things about manual bidding is that you must make sure that you're priming your metrics ahead of starting that so the better your click-through rate is and your conversion rate than the lower your CPC as well you know one of the things about manual bid is as you start to bid higher generally your costs will go up but also and this is something I find time and time again as your costs go up often your click-through rate
